[patient_name] is a 8u year ola maie with pmh: - small cell neuroendocrine tumor (limited staging, s/p chemotherapy, in remission) - copd - hfref (ef 31%) patient presented to ccag on [encounter_date] with complaints of shortness of breath. patient reports for the last several weeks he has been having shortness of breath, however it has acutely worsened over the last week. he is normally able to walk to the bathroom without difficulty, but has had trouble doing that due to exertional dyspnea. he has had to use his rescue inhaler 6 times a day. he notes that last night his symptoms acutely worsen and he was not able to get out of his chair due to difficulty breathing. this morning he was noted to be hypoxemic to 82%. he has never been hypoxemic to that level in the past. he denies dyspnea at rest, only with exertion. he was admitted to the hospital in december for pneumonia, and was treated for pneumonia in january. he smoked for 50 years, however quit in july 2023 after receiving his cancer diagnosis. he also quit alcohol consumption at that time and denies any other substance use. on transport he received duonebs and was placed on nonrebreather. on arrival bp 136/79, heart rate 129, spo2 91%, respiratory rate 36. ekg notable for prolonged qtc 528. he was placed on bipap and improved with solumedrol 125mg and duonebs. he was given doxcycline. he was weaned off bipap and was initially admitted to the regular nursing floor. however he began to experience significant shortness of breath with increased work of breathing with any movement and requested bipap be placed back on. he was admitted to micu for acute respiratory failure requiring bipap. on workup, covid/flu/rsv negative. cr 1.85

[patient_name] is a 8u year ola maie with pmh: - small cell neuroendocrine tumor (limited staging, s/p chemotherapy, in remission) - copd - hfref (ef 31%) patient presented to ccag on [encounter_date] with complaints of shortness of breath. patient reports for the last several weeks he has been having shortness of breath, however it has acutely worsened over the last week. he is normally able to walk to the bathroom without difficulty, but has had trouble doing that due to exertional dyspnea. he has had to use his rescue inhaler 6 times a day. he notes that last night his symptoms acutely worsen and he was not able to get out of his chair due to difficulty breathing. this morning he was noted to be hypoxemic to 82%. he has never been hypoxemic to that level in the past. he denies dyspnea at rest, only with exertion. he was admitted to the hospital in december for pneumonia, and was treated for pneumonia in january. he smoked for 50 years, however quit in july 2023 after receiving his cancer diagnosis. he also quit alcohol consumption at that time and denies any other substance use. on transport he received duonebs and was placed on nonrebreather. on arrival bp 136/79, heart rate 129, spo2 91%, respiratory rate 36. ekg notable for prolonged qtc 528. he was placed on bipap and improved with solumedrol 125mg and duonebs. he was given doxcycline. he was weaned off bipap and was initially admitted to the regular nursing floor. however he began to experience significant shortness of breath with increased work of breathing with any movement and requested bipap be placed back on. he was admitted to micu for acute respiratory failure requiring bipap. on workup, covid/flu/rsv negative. cr 1.85
